title: clerodendrum ternatum var. lanceolatum
intro: Clerodendrum ternatum var. lanceolatum, also known as the East Indian glory bower, is a tropical vine native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia, characterized by its beautiful white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been utilized in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Clerodendrum ternatum var. lanceolatum include its potential to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, with caution advised due to potential skin irritation and allergic reactions.
